Question: Calculate the arc length of the parameterized curve $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 2 t^{2} + 1 , 2 t^{2} - 1 , t^{3} \rangle , 0 \le t \le 3.$$

Find the arc-length function for the helix $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 3 \text{cos} t , 3 \text{sin} t , 4 t \rangle , t \geq 0.$$ Then, use the relationship between the arc length and the parameter t to find an arc-length parameterization of $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) .$$
Answer: $$s = 5 t ,$$ or $$t = s \text{/} 5 .$$ Substituting this into $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 3 \text{cos} t , 3 \text{sin} t , 4 t \rangle$$ gives $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( s ) = \langle 3 \text{cos} ( \frac{s}{5} ) , 3 \text{sin} ( \frac{s}{5} ) , \frac{4 s}{5} \rangle , s \geq 0.$$

Question: Find the curvature of the curve defined by the function $$y = 3 x^{2} - 2 x + 4$$ at the point $$x = 2.$$
Find the unit normal vector for the vector-valued function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( t^{2} - 3 t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + ( 4 t + 1 ) \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ and evaluate it at $$t = 2.$$
Find the equation of the osculating circle of the curve defined by the vector-valued function $$y = 2 x^{2} - 4 x + 5$$ at $$x = 1.$$
Answer: $$\kappa = \frac{4}{[ 1 + ( 4 x - 4 )^{2} ]^{3 \text{/} 2}}$$ At the point $$x = 1 ,$$ the curvature is equal to 4. Therefore, the radius of the osculating circle is $$\frac{1}{4} .$$ A graph of this function appears next: The vertex of this parabola is located at the point $$( 1 , 3 ) .$$ Furthermore, the center of the osculating circle is directly above the vertex. Therefore, the coordinates of the center are $$( 1 , \frac{13}{4} ) .$$ The equation of the osculating circle is $$( x - 1 )^{2} + ( y - \frac{13}{4} )^{2} = \frac{1}{16} .$$
